text: Meet Argo. One command turns a Playwright script into a launch-ready product video, complete with A.I. voiceover and camera direction.
The pipeline is three steps. Write the demo, record the browser, export the video. Camera effects like zoom-to guide the viewer through each step automatically.
Four pillars make it work. Overlays for on-screen text. Voiceover with your choice of engine. Camera effects for editorial direction. And a pipeline that exports everything at once.

Pick your voice. Six engines from local to cloud. Or clone your own voice with M.L.X. audio — record a fifteen second clip, and every demo sounds like you. Locally, privately, no data leaves your machine.
Four camera effects. Spotlight. Focus ring. Dim-around. And zoom-to. Each one is a single line in your demo script.
Your demo scripts are just Playwright tests with a few extra helpers. Spotlight, focus ring, dim-around, zoom-to, confetti — all one-liners in your script.
Overlays auto-detect your page theme and adapt. The pipeline also generates subtitles, chapter markers, and a scene timing report alongside every video.
Oh, and this demo? It was recorded by Argo, using Argo.
